entry.title: Real Talk with Suna Singh | Track Lead for Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members 👋

I am Suna Singh, an Operations Expert at Cloud ERP and one of my passions is with SAP TechEd. Beyond that, I like travelling around the world to meet new people and cultures and embrace new adventures. I also love skiing and reading.   

In the track “Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP” you will explore how SAP S/4HANA Cloud can enhance your digital transformation by supporting your business needs from mission-critical operations to business-model innovation. Whether adopting your first ERP or migrating from an existing one to the cloud, you can gain a complete, modular solution with the AI and analytics capabilities through exploring our GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP solutions. Learn ways to extend and embed SAP S/4HANA Cloud and use the power of AI and automation.

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

To support your digital transformation, we provide a broad range of topics such as transitioning paths to SAP S/4HANA Cloud including tools and services, extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, architecture, clean core, AI, user experience, security, and many more. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

Our experts will not only talk about the latest innovations, they will also showcase various demos and share best practices with you.  

In the Jump-Start sessions - DT180 Integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP IBP using SAP Integration Suite and DT280 Build clean core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - you will get the opportunity to gain hands-on experience and complete the exercises at your own pace.  

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar) 

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future: Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. 
  • DT101 | Choosing the right path for your business to SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Find out about the important factors, explore the right option for your organization, and get insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. 
  • DT102 | Tools and services to make your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud easier: Discover tools and strategies for your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the RISE with SAP solution. 
  • DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Learn about key user and development tools in the ABAP Cloud development model and how customers and partners can manage their on-stack extensions to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.  
  • DT103 | SAP Business AI in cloud ERP: Understand how your business processes are supported by cloud ERP solutions from SAP with the product portfolio enhanced with AI and other intelligent technologies, including traditional machine learning, large language models, generative AI, and situation handling.  

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Agenda Builder: Build your own agenda in order not to miss any session you are interested in. 
  • Explore: Explore our session offering, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. 
  • Inspiration: Join us for a high-energy, interactive virtual event experience and get inspired. 

Want to learn more about the track Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP? Explore the  TechEd 2024 session catalog and ensure your favorite track sessions are on your list.  

Don’t miss the event and and sign up today!

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2024 (issue #30)
entry.content:
September marks the start of autumn, which means back to school for our kids and conference season for us. Both are focused on learning new things, which is so important in our industry where technology changes so quickly.

Some events to look for include Devtoberfest, SAP TechEd, and the next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg.

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group. Our next meeting is Wednesday, October 9, 2024. You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

How to create an SAP Fiori elements application from within ADT

This integration helps bridge the gaps between the different development environments that are currently required to build an SAP Fiori elements app and deploy it on an ABAP stack.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-sap-fiori-elements-application-from-within-adt/ba-p/13797232

Side Effects in SAP Fiori elements apps: triggering actions and updating data in your unmanaged RAP application Side Effects refer to system behaviors where a user’s action, such as modifying a field or performing a specific task, triggers changes in other parts of the user interface. For instance, updating a field might automatically adjust related fields or data sections. These changes are crucial for maintaining data consistency and ensuring that the user interface reflects the latest information.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/side-effects-in-fiori-triggering-actions-and-updating-data-in-your/ba-p/13851571

How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment

All SAP Fiori elements apps provide a download to Excel as a standard feature. However, only a few apps support uploading from a spreadsheet. In this series, you will see how to upload records from a spreadsheet file.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13785946 (part 1)
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13800792 (part 2)

Smart and flexible: sap.ui.mdc library is released for productive use in SAPUI5 1.124

sap.ui.mdc helps to build enterprise-ready smart controls or applications, no matter which back-end technology and data protocol you are using. It offers a flexible and comprehensive reuse approach with implementable delegates for the sap.ui.mdc controls. Using specific protocols like FHIR or even a plain REST service is now possible without having to start from scratch.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-and-flexible-sap-ui-mdc-released-for-productive-use/ba-p/13793439

SAP BTP ABAP environment – release 2408

A summary of updates to ADT, CDS, RAP, and other innovations for ABAP developers.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

Events

Devtoberfest starts September 23

Devtoberfest aims to capture the experience of live pre-conference events, like SAP InnoJam of years’ past. It officially kicks off the SAP TechEd season. Devtoberfest takes some of the best aspects of the traditional hackathon type event, such as learning by building something, but opens the experience to the entire community and spreads across multiple weeks to better accommodate everyone’s busy schedules.
https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-welcome/ba-p/9358

SAP TechEd runs October 8-9

User experience and app dev sessions at SAP TechEd 2024

For 2024, SAP TechEd is virtual once again, with lots of great sessions grouped into six content tracks. If you prefer in-person learning, SAP TechEd on Tour consists of companion events around the world in collaboration with regional user groups (ASUG, SAUG, UKISUG, and DSAG).

Here are some sessions on UX and application development.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/user-experience-and-app-dev-sessions-at-sap-teched-2024-virtual/ba-p/13864322

SAPUI5 at SAP TechEd: tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
See AD101 in the session catalog

Full TechEd session catalog

Content tracks – we recommend you check the application development and automation tracks

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg on November 19

After our successful events in Dublin and Chicago, we are bringing SAP Fiori Innovation Day back to Europe. The next one will be in Heidelberg on November 19. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

Register today:
https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home

Summary of SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago

If you want to gain a sense of what we do in SAP Fiori Innovation Day and what you might learn, you can read the summary of our event in Chicago.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-innovation-day-in-chicago-sap-fiori-fundamentals-ux/ba-p/13797787

Tutorials

openSAP content has moved to the learning.sap.com platform

Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps
https://learning.sap.com/courses/developing-and-extending-sap-fiori-elements-apps

Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service

Getting Started with Creating an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on an OData V4 RAP Service

This new learning journey guides you through building SAP Fiori elements applications from scratch using a RAP OData V4 service.
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/getting-started-with-creating-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-an-odata-v4-rap-service

Adapting the UI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Screen Personas

https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas

Tutorial: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 applications

To extend SAPUI5 applications, SAPUI5 Flexibility offers capabilities not only for developers, but also for key users and end users using simple visual / WYSIWYG tools.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-tutorial-mission-key-user-adaptation-for-sapui5-applications/ba-p/13639694

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers. See this blog post linking to an extensive, end-to-end tutorial:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extending-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-using-sapui5-adaptation-projects/ba-p/13607813

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)

SAP FIori development newsletter #28 (May 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #27 (March 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #26 (January 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #25 (November 2023)

entry.title: Plan your agenda for TechEd Virtual
entry.content:
It’s time to start planning your agenda for SAP TechEd Virtual! Head over to the TechEd Virtual dashboard to begin organizing your sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/dashboard/page/dashboard

To help you get started, here are a few recommendations that might be useful:

Keynote & Developer Keynote

By default, the Keynote is automatically added to your list of scheduled sessions. This session offers a high-level overview of the latest innovations and cool new developments from SAP. It also provides valuable insights into SAP's future strategy.

However, don’t overlook the Developer Keynote! This is another essential session to include in your TechEd Virtual agenda. Unlike the main keynote, the Developer Keynote is all about diving deep into the technical aspects. If you're keen on hands-on technical insights and want a closer look at the latest tools and developments, this session is a must!

 

Prioritize your interests

Next, start by applying a filter to narrow down the list of sessions to those that align with your interests. This should be your priority, focusing on sessions that offer updates and new insights in areas you’re passionate about or working on. Once you’ve locked in these key sessions, you can fill any gaps in your schedule with other sessions that catch your eye or topics you’d like to explore further.

Personally, I selected the following tracks as a filter:

 

Maximize your schedule

Once you’ve selected all the sessions that match your interests, it’s time to fill any gaps in your schedule. Head to your dashboard and identify where those gaps are in your list of scheduled sessions. Then, browse the session catalog to find additional sessions that fit those time slots. Choose the ones that seem most interesting or relevant to you.

For example, I had a gap between 10:30 am and 11:00 am on the first day:

Remove all the filters:

And look at the sessions available at that time. I filled this gap with the Keynote recap + Community welcome:

 

Plan backup sessions

It's a good idea to plan backup sessions in case your scheduled session doesn't meet your expectations. By having an alternative ready, you can easily switch if needed. While scheduling a backup session at the same time will create a conflict, you can simply keep the one you prefer and mark the backup as a favorite. This allows you to quickly jump to it if needed, or catch the replay afterward.

Many sessions will focus on Joule, both as an AI assistant for our business and as a powerful tool for developers. I’m really excited to explore all the new innovations Joule has to offer. It’s definitely something to keep an eye on during SAP TechEd Virtual! (though it will be hard to miss! 😊)

Be sure to join the SAP TechEd community group to connect and network with fellow community members. Also, don’t forget to sign up for Devtoberfest for even more learning and fun!

 

If you’re feeling overwhelmed by virtual sessions, consider attending SAPInsider EMEA in Copenhagen, which is partnering with TechEd on Tour: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024. Tom Cenens and I will be presenting a session on BTP titled “Key Strategies for Effective SAP BTP Administration and Governance.” It’s a great opportunity to connect in person and dive deep into BTP strategies!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 20th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 23rd.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the first week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  We are kicking things off there with the ever-popular Petoberfest. 

List of activities for contest week 1:

entry.title: Real Talk with Shilpa & Ananth | Integration Track Leads
entry.content:
We are Shilpa Vij and Ananth Natrajan, Product Managers for SAP BTP Integration Suite. We look forward to the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual, especially the SAP BTP Integration track. 

Shilpa Vij: I am a passionate dancer and home decor enthusiast, and by profession, I am a Product Manager. I have co-authored two books. Currently, my interest lies in exploring, learning, and showcasing "product management" as an art.  

Ananth Natarajan: I’m a great enthusiast of nature and love every creature of nature. Apart from my Product Manager role in the SAP Integration Suite team, I prefer to travel and explore different places within my reach. 

The Integration track provides an in-depth overview of SAP's powerful integration tools and solutions, focusing on seamlessly connecting applications, data, business partners, and processes across diverse environments. This track covers topics such as event-driven architecture, API management, hybrid integration, and migration strategies, enabling businesses to drive agility, efficiency, and innovation. Through expert-led sessions, attendees will explore real-world use cases and best practices, gaining valuable insights into leveraging SAP Integration Suite to support digital transformation and optimize business outcomes. 

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

Explore a diverse array of topics, including AI in Enterprise Integration, API Management, Event-driven integration, Integration Advisor, Trading Partner Management, Migration Accelerators to move to the Cloud, Edge Integration Cell, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and beyond. We’re thrilled to work with such exceptional speakers and product managers. Stay tuned for exciting updates and feature releases that we'll be sharing at TechEd! 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

Through jump-start sessions, real-world case studies, and interactive discussions, participants learn how to bring their integration ideas to life using the SAP Integration Suite. The track focuses on practical, scalable approaches that empower businesses to solve complex integration challenges, streamline operations, and enable seamless connectivity across cloud and on-premise environments—turning visionary ideas into impactful realities. 

Stay tuned for jumpstart sessions IN180 Future-proof your SAP PI/PO investments with SAP Integration Suite & IN280 Use SAP Integration Suite with SAP IBP and third-party systems

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar):  

  • IN200 | Unleash the power of AI in enterprise integrations 
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
  • IN101 | Leverage real-time business insights with an event-driven architecture 
  • IN102 | Take control of your APIs with the API Management and Graph capabilities 
  • IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions 
  • IN202 | Gain flexibility and scalability with a hybrid integration platform
  • IN201 | Dive into the B2B world of integration
  • IN106 | Ferring’s integration journey from legacy to SAP Integration Suit
  • IN104 | Design and run your integrations smoothly with SAP Integration Suite
  • IN196 | The increased usage of SAP Cloud ALM in integration scenarios

To gain an overview of the SAP Integration Suite, including the latest innovations and roadmap, we encourage you to attend the session IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 

 What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • A coffee cup for early morning keynotes! 
  • The device of choice: Whether it’s your laptop, tablet, or phone, you can tune into TechEd from wherever you are this year! 
  • Bring a spirit of exploration and eagerness to learn: TechEd is your chance to dive into cutting-edge technologies. Be ready to connect with SAP experts, ask questions, and soak up all the knowledge you can!  

Join us in the Integration track and be part of a vibrant community eager to explore the future of seamless connectivity. Dive into sessions that will equip you with the tools and knowledge to master the SAP Integration Suite and discover how it can elevate your business processes. Don't miss this opportunity to engage with experts, exchange ideas, and learn from hands-on demos. Register now and let's unlock new possibilities together! Explore TechEd 2024 session catalog!

entry.title: Real Talk with Katya and Jerome | Artificial Intelligence Track Leads
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

We’re Katya Reznik and Jerome Simonetti, and we’re excited to introduce ourselves and give you a glimpse of what to expect from the AI track sessions at this year’s TechEd. 

Katya: Originally from Russia, I’ve lived in Israel for the past 9 years. I love wandering in the desert, snowboarding, diving in cenotes and seas, and exploring nature. I travel a lot, learn languages, make pottery, enjoy the rain, love all dogs and people with a sense of humor. I’m part of the Business AI unit and often talk about technologies and our future:) 

Jerome: I’m Product Expert at SAP Business AI Product Management team. I like to go on multi-day hikes with my wife. My vacation is a subtle balance between visiting cities and museums and going hiking. In terms of art, I'm quite eclectic. I love music (jazz, lyrical, opera) and I regularly go to concerts. I love painting, especially the Italian grandmasters of the 15th and 16th century.  I also like comics. I am married. I have 3 children who are adults. 2 of them have children! 

With our AI track you will learn how innovations like Joule, our generative AI copilot, and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming key areas such as finance, HR, supply chain, and IT. Discover real-world success stories, expert insights, and actionable ways to enhance decision-making and agility in your organization. 

We look forward to sharing insights and innovations in AI with you at TechEd! 

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

The transformative potential of AI and how it can revolutionize the way businesses operate is truly fascinating. It’s an exciting opportunity to dive deep into topics of generative AI, machine learning, knowledge graphs and much more. Specializing in this track allows us to explore/witness real-world success stories and expert insights. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

This track perfectly captures the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit by offering both hands-on experience and real-world applications. The two jump-start sessions bring AI from theory to practical implementation: Set up Joule to answer your own policy documents and Build your own generative AI scenario for your use case. Additionally, our sessions with customers like Sappi and the City of Hamburg and a partner MSG showcase how AI is already driving innovation and delivering results in real business environments. 

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar): 

  • AI100 - Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results 
  • AI105 - Examining how to onboard and extend Joule 
  • AI106 - Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub 
  • AI104 - Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot 
  • AI108 - How AI agents can support your business in the future 
  • AI102 - Enhance business predictions with SAP Foundation Model and knowledge graphs 
  • AI101 - Enable more secure and responsible generative AI 

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Curiosity & Questions: Most importantly, come with an open mind and questions. TechEd is about learning, so actively participate in sessions, ask the experts, and explore new ideas. 
  • Time Management: With so many sessions, it's easy to get overwhelmed. Build your agenda and don’t forget to schedule breaks to keep yourself fresh and engaged throughout the day. 
  • Comfortable Workspace ️: Since TechEd is virtual, set up a comfortable and quiet workspace that helps you focus. A good chair and reliable internet are important to stay connected throughout the sessions. 

We can’t wait to have you join us at TechEd Virtual! Together, we’ll explore how AI can transform business operations and create real impact. Register now, and let’s shape the future of AI together! We’re excited to engage with you, answer your questions, and learn from each other along the way. See you there!

entry.title: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP and Microsoft have announced their collaboration to modernize identity and access management (IAM) for SAP customers. SAP recommends its SAP Identity Management (IDM) customers transition to Microsoft Entra (formerly known as Microsoft Azure Active Directory) as the SAP IDM solution approaches the end of maintenance by 2027, with extended maintenance until 2030. This blog post series aims to provide technical guidance on how to plan and implement IAM for SAP applications with Microsoft Entra following SAP's reference architecture for Cloud-leading identity lifecycle.

Scenario overview

This blog post series focuses on key scenarios, starting with the implementation of a workflow-based access management solution to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) with Microsoft Entra ID Governance.

📢 📢 📢  Note
Don't miss SAP TechEd 2024 and check out session XP202, Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra, where @RegineSchimmer and I will focus on this scenario and show a live demo.

Figure 1 illustrates the scenario in more detail:

Figure 1: Access management to BTP with Entra ID Governance

The developer (John Doe) requires access to SAP Business Application Studio which serves as an example for an SAP BTP application in this scenario. To work with SAP Business Application Studio, John's user must be a assigned to the Developer role in the BTP subaccount, which is part of the Business_Application_Studio_Developer role collection that gets automatically created with the subscription to SAP Business Application Studio. Granting the access must be approved by the Entra ID administrator (Jane Smith).

To implement this workflow for managing the access to BTP, the following entities in Entra are required:

  • All users in the Entra tenant working with SAP Business Application Studio must be a member of the SAP BTP Developer Group. This is a group of type Security. 
  • The SAP BTP Developer Group is assigned to the SAP BTP Access Package. Access Packages are a feature of Entitlement Management in Microsoft Entra ID Governance that allows administrators to bundle resources such as groups, applications, and SharePoint Online sites into a single package. This package can then be assigned to users based on policies that define who can request access, approval workflows, and lifecycle settings. In this scenario, John requests his membership in the SAP BTP Developer Group by starting a workflow for the SAP BTP Access Package that Jane as an Administrator for the package can approve.

If John's request gets approved by Jane and he becomes a member of the Entra ID security group SAP BTP Developer, how is this information securely transferred to SAP BTP? Microsoft Entra ID issues security tokens to successfully authenticated users, such as access tokens, refresh tokens, and ID tokens. Depending on the token type they contain different information, expressed as name-value pairs (also known as claims), that reliably provide facts about the user's identity. Claims can include details like the user's unique ID, email address, the user's department, job title, and other attributes related to the user's role within the organization. To securely transfer John's membership in the SAP BTP Developer group to BTP, Entra can be configured to include John's group memberships as a claim in the ID token. Since different applications usually require different sets of claims in the tokens, customization of the claims is done on an application registration for IAS (SAP Cloud Identity Services Application Registration in figure 1) in the Entra tenant.

When John opens the URL to SAP Business Application Studio in an browser, he first gets redirected to the trusted identity provider (IDP) in the BTP subaccount to authenticate. In BTP, this task is delegated to an Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant of the SAP Cloud Identity Services using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) protocol. Since the IAS tenant is not aware of John's memberships in Entra security groups, it forwards the application's OIDC authorization request to Entra ID by acting as an OIDC proxy. This requires to configure the Entra ID tenant as a Corporate IDP in IAS which causes John to sign-in to Entra instead of IAS. Upon successful authentication, Entra sends the OIDC authentication response back to IAS, containing an authorization code to request the ID and access tokens from Entra.

To identify the IAS tenant as a legitimate client when sending the OIDC token request, Entra requires IAS to authenticate. OIDC defines several authentication methods for clients, such as a client secret, which is a shared secret with a configurable expiration date (maximum 24 months) generated by Entra and shared with IAS. The drawback of using a client secret is that it is sent every time when IAS performs client authentication to obtain the tokens from Entra. When the secret is about to reach its expiration, the secret must be rotated in Entra ID and shared again with the administrator of the IAS tenant.

To reduce the administrative overhead and avoid sending a shared secret, OIDC also supports client authentication based on an asymmetric key pair. Here, the IAS tenant as the OIDC (proxy) client generates a JSON Web Token (JWT) according to RFC 7532 and uses its private key to sign it. The corresponding public key certificate is shared with the Entra tenant to establish the trust relationship. Similar to the claim configuration of the issued tokens, trust to the IAS JWT signing certificate is configured in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Application Registration. 

Now that the information about John's membership in the Entra security group SAP BTP Developer can be securely transferred to BTP when John authenticates with IAS on BTP, one question still remains opens: How is John assigned to the Developer role in BTP if he is a member of the SAP BTP Developer group in Entra? Let's have a look at John's OAuth JWT access token issued by Entra ID in the token response to the IAS token request: 

Figure 2: Entra ID Access Token

John's membership to the SAP BTP Developer group is emitted as a claim with the name groups that contains an array of GUIDs (globally unique identifiers). Each GUID is the value of the Security group's object identifier (OID) attribute in Microsoft Entra. In BTP, a role collection can be assigned to a user group by mapping the group(s) OID(s) to the role collection. This approach follows the recommendations for federated authorization management between Entra and IAS, and will be configured in the following steps.

Prerequisites

To follow the steps in this tutorial, make sure you have access to the following services:

  • An IAS tenant with full administrative access. You can request an IAS trial instance as described here for your BTP trial account.
  • An SAP BTP subaccount with Space Manager access. A free trial account will work for this scenario. The IAS tenant is already configured as a trusted identity provider for the applications in this subaccount.
  • Administrator-level access to an Microsoft Entra ID P2 subscription. You can obtain a P2 tenant for development and learning purposes with a free Microsoft 365 E5 developer subscription. With this subscription you can request a trial of Microsoft Entra ID Governance by following these steps.
  • Two test users in your Entra ID tenant, one for requesting the access to BTP, and the other for approving it.

Register SAP Cloud Identity Services application in Entra

Let's get started with creating the application registration for SAP Cloud Identity Services and configuring the token claims for it.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
1

Login to the Entra admin center with your Entra administrator. Go to Identity -> Applications -> App Registrations.

Click New registration.

2

Enter a name, e.g. "SAP Cloud Identity Services".

Click Register.

3Copy the Application (client) ID from the Overview to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.
4Click Endpoints.
5

Copy the OpenID Connect metadata document URL to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.

Close the dialog window.

6

Select Token configuration from the menu.

Click Add groups claim.

7

Activate the Security groups checkbox.

Click Add.

OIDC corporate IDP configuration in IAS

The data captured from the app registration in Entra is now required to configure the OIDC proxy settings for the corporate IDP in IAS.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
8

Open a new tab in the browser and login to the Administration Console of your SAP CIS IAS tenant at  https://<ias-tenant-name>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin

Go to Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers.

9Click Create.
10

Enter a Display Name, e.g. "BestRun Corp. Entra ID Tenant".

Select OpenID Connect Compliant for the Identity Provider Type.

Click Create.

11For the newly created Corportate Identity Provider, select OpenID Connect Configuration from the Trust tab.
12

Paste the URL copied in step 5 into the Discovery URL field.

Click Load.

13From the Client Authentication Method drop-down list, select Private key JWT.
14Paste the value of the Application (client) ID copied in step 3 into the Client ID field.
15Scroll down to the Scopes section and click Add.
16

Enter offline_access for the new scope value.

Click Save.

17

Scroll up and click Save.

Then, click OIDC Callback URL to copy the value into the clipboard.

18From the navigation menu, select Applications & Resources -> Tenant Settings
19On the Authentication tab, go to your tenant's OpenID Connect Configuration.
20Click the Download button of the default Signing Certificate.
21From the navigation menu, select Applications & Resources -> Applications
22

Select your SAP BTP subaccount listed under Bundled Applications.

Select Conditional Authentication from the SAP BTP subaccount's Trust settings.

23

From the Default Identity Provider drop-down list, select the corporate identity provider created earlier.

Click Save.

24Select Attributes from the SAP BTP subaccount's Trust settings.
25Click Add.
26

In the Name field of the new attribute, enter "groups".

Select "Corporate Identity Provider" as the Source.

Enter "${corporateIdP.groups}" in the Value field.

This additional attribute setting forwards the value of the groups claim in the Entra ID token to the BTP applications in the SAP BTP subaccount.

Click Save.

Complete the Application Registration in Entra

To complete the OIDC proxy setup between Entra ID and IAS, the trust relationship must be established by uploading the IAS signing certificate to Entra and registering the IAS callback URL.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
27

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Go to Manage -> Authentication. 

Click Add Platform.

28Select Web.
29

Paste the OIDC Callback URL copied in step 17 into the Redirect URIs field.

Click Configure.

30

Go to Manage -> Certificates & Secrets.

On the Certificates tab, click Upload certificate.

31

Open the file upload dialog and select the certificate file you downloaded in step 20.

Click Add.

32The IAS signing cerificate is now listed under the Certificates tab.

Validate the OIDC Proxy Setup

Now that the OIDC proxy setup is completed in Entra, you can validate the configuration in IAS.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
33

Go back to the IAS Administration console browser tab.

Navigate to Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers in the menu.

34

Select your corporate identity provider from the list and select OpenID Connect Configuration.

Click Validate.

35

Accept the requested permissions from the Entra SAP Cloud Identity Services app.

You may also activate the checkbox to consent on behalf-of your organization.

36You can now inspect the token sent by Entra to IAS and see for example the groups claim and its values for your user.

Create the user group in Entra

Continue the setup in the Entra Admin center by creating the group for SAP BTP Developers.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
37

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Navigate to Identity -> Groups.

Click New Group.

38

Select Security for the Group type.

Enter a Group name, e.g. "SAP BTP Developer".

Select Assigned from the Membership type drop-down list.

Click Create.

39Select the newly created group from the list.
40Copy the Object ID to the clipboard and paste the value into a text file for later use.

Assign the group to the BTP role collection

Lorem ipsum

StepDescriptionScreenshot
41

In a new browser tab, login to your BTP subaccount in the BTP Cloud Cockpit. For a trial account, use this link.

Select your subaccount.

42

Navigate to Security -> Role collections.

Select the Business_Application_Studio_Developer role collection from the list.

Click Edit.

43Under User Groups, select the IAS tenant as the identity provider, and paste the Object ID from the Entra ID group you copied in step 40.

Create the Access Package

Now it is time to configure the workflow for requesting and approving access to BTP by creating the access package in Entra ID Governance entitlement management.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
44

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Navigate to Identity Governance -> Entitlement management -> Access Packages.

Click New access package.

45

Enter a Name, e.g. "SAP BTP", and provide a short Description.

You can leave the assignment of the new access package to the General catalog.

Click Next: Resource roles.

46Click Groups and Teams.
47

Activate the checkbox to see all Groups and Teams not in the General catalog.

In the search field, enter the name of the group you created, e.g. "SAP BTP Developer"

Activate the checkbox of the group from the search results list.

Click Select.

48Click Next: Requests.
49Select For users in your directory and allow All members (excluding guests) therein to make a request for the access package.
50

Scroll down to the Approval section. 

Choose Yes to Require approval.

For the First Approver, select Choose specific approvers from the drop-down list.

Click Add approvers.

51Search for your user who will approve the access request and click Select.
52

Choose Yes to Enable new requests.

Click Next: Requestor information.

53Click Next: Lifecycle.
54Click Next: Rules.
55Click Next: Review + create.
56Click Create.
57Copy the link to MyAccess portal to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.

Test access to BTP without the assignment

Now you are ready to test the scenario! Let's start with verifying that access is still denied for your test user.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
58

Go back to the BTP Cloud Cockpit browser tab.

Navigate to your subaccount's Services -> Instances and Subscriptions.

Copy the URL to the SAP Business Application Studio to the clipboard.

59

Open a new private browser window.

Paste the URL you copied in the previous step to your SAP Business Application Studio instance from the clipboard into the browser and hit Enter.

You are redirected by the IAS tenant to Entra ID as the corporate IDP for your BTP subaccount's business applications.

Login as the test user who will request access to SAP Business Application Studio.

60You see a message that the user has no access yet due to the missing assignment to the Developer role.

Request the access package

StepDescriptionScreenshot
61

Start the request workflow in a new tab of the private browser window by opening the URL to the MyAccess portal you copied in step 58.

Click Continue.

62Click Submit request.
63

You receive a confirmation message that your request to access the SAP BTP package is being processed.

Close the private browser window.

Approve the request

Now you take over the role as the approver.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
64

Re-open a private browser window. Start the approval workflow by opening the URL to the MyAccess portal you copied in step 57.

Login as the user who you selected in step 51 for approving requests of the SAP BTP access package.

65

Naviate to Approvals in the MyAccess portal.

Select your other test user's access request from the list of Pending approvals.

Click Approve.

66Click Submit.
67

You receive a confirmation message you approved the request.

Close the private browser window.

Test access with the assignment

Now let's try again to open SAP Business Application Studio as the test user with the approved access request.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
68

Re-open a private browser window. Go to the URL of the SAP Business Application Studio you copied in step 58.

You are redirected by the IAS tenant to Entra ID as the corporate IDP for your BTP subaccount's business applications.

Login as the test user who has requested access to SAP Business Application Studio.

69This time the user can successfully load and use the SAP Business Application Studio.

With this last step you've successfully completed the scenario and I hope you enjoyed it!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 26th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/26/sap-developer-news-september-26th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Extensibility Wizard

  • Blog by Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, overview of Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • Blog by Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, on side-by-side extensions with SAP Build process Automation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/creating-side-by-side-process-extensions-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13867657
  • Documentation:
    • Setup on BTP: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
    • Setup on S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b2ee5784ccfc45d5acf42d4c60eb5270.html
  • Antonio Maradiaga’s videos on setting up event mesh for SAP Build Process Automation:
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Delivering events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Process Automation via AEM
  • Devtoberfest Session: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/s-4hana-extensions-start-fast-with-the-new-extensibility-wizard/ev-p/13795541
  • Petoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/petoberfest-2024/m-p/13872559

Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 1: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-1-contest-activities/ba-p/13872705
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview

  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: Coming Soon
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd Community Organized Watch Party

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG
  • SAP Community Watch Zurich: https://community.sap.com/t5/z%C3%BCrich-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-zurich/ec-p/13873047#M1
  • SAP Community Watch Hamburg: https://community.sap.com/t5/hamburg-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-hamburg/ev-p/13875609
  • SAP Community Watch Sao Paulo: https://community.sap.com/t5/s%C3%A3o-paulo-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-s%C3%A3o-paulo/ec-p/13875927#M3
  • SAP Inside Track Bengaluru: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-5th-october-2024/ev-p/13855659

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Extensibility Wizard
2:09 Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap
4:03 Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview
6:56 SAP TechEd Community Watch Party

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 26, 2024.

[Daniel and Pepper] For Petoberfest, I'm here with Pepper, and we're here to talk about the Extensibility Wizard, which was released last week and let users create extensions right from within the UI of their SAP app. For the first release, it will work in S/4HANA Cloud and will let you create custom logic, custom fields known as on stack extensions or custom or extension processes known as side-by-side extensions. [Don't go to sleep.]

One of the big benefits is that it's context aware. So for example, if I'm in the sales order application, it will offer me sales order related event triggers to go with my process. There's a little bit of setup involved. So your S/4 HANA Cloud has to know about your SAP BTP and SAP Build installations. That's fairly simple, just involves destinations, trust and communication arrangements. You also have to set up Event Mesh so that your events from S/4 HANA can arrive to process automation.

Luckily, my esteemed colleague Antonio Larediaga has already created two videos to show you very simply how to set this up. One to get your events from S/4 HANA into Event Mesh and then from Event Mesh to Process Automation. The extensibility wizard will undoubtedly be the talk of TechEd and that's why we've set up a Devtoberfest session where Sesh and Kartik can talk at length about how you set this up and how How do you use it? And you will be able to ask them questions. I will put links to all of the videos, the blogs, and the Devtoberfest session in the description.

[Say goodbye.] Bye-bye. 🐶

[Thomas] It seems like just the other day we were starting our planning for this year's Devtoberfest. And here we are, nearly at the end of the first week of the four-week event. My how's time flown. Our team has been super busy both preparing for SAP TechEd virtual, TechEd on tour, as well as delivering these great Devtoberfest sessions to you. At the end of the first week, we've had a really packed week, great attendance. Up over last year, some more people are finding their way to Devtoberfest, which makes us very happy. We've had some excellent sessions across all the topic days.

Keep in mind that Monday is ABAP and CAP day. Tuesday is our day for tooling, Tooling Tuesday, where we're looking at SAP Build, Build Apps, Build Code, Build Process Automation, and how all these tools are bringing innovations for CAP development, low-code, no-code development, and ABAP development as well. Wednesday is our Integration Day. Thursday is MAD, our Machine Learning, AI, and Data Day. And then finally, Friday is both our Front-end Friday, which looks at SAP UI5 and Fiori and other topics related to front-end tools. But Friday is also our Fun Friday, where we have special activities.

Depending upon when you're watching this news item, There may still be time to enter into Petoberfest. Uh, that is our Fun Friday activity for September 27th. Post a picture of your pet in your work environment and, and you can earn some bonus points as well as have fun checking out everybody else's pet pictures as well.

Next Friday, we're going to have another special event. It's going to be our Techtoberfest day on, uh, October the 4th. And on that day, we're going to be asking you to post pictures of your work environment. Show us your laptop set up, your desk set up, whatever cool kind of thing you have set up in your work environment.

Hey, in week two, we will repeat the same daily track structure that you've that you've experienced in this first week. In fact, all four weeks will follow the same track structure, but our Monday is particularly packed with eight individual sessions across CAP and ABAP. We've got some really great things coming in that day. And it's going to be chock full of content. On Tuesday, I'm particularly interested to see the Build Apps, what's coming soon, where they're going to show both new AI innovations and the introduction of SAP UI5 output for Build Apps. Something that I've personally been looking for, for a while. We've got some cool stuff on the integration day around the event architecture and cloud events. We have content on the mad day around generative AI and using CAP with generative AI as well. So a real generative AI kind of day for that particular Thursday. And then finally, for our front end day, we have building elegant applications with Fiori for and Build Code for Android and an iOS Building applications for iOS as well. So we really have a mobile day focused on both Android and iOS

So we've got quite a selection of content for you all throughout the week and keep in mind once we finish week two we'll go directly into TechEd Virtual brought to you live from Germany And then we'll come back with more Devtoberfest, two more weeks of Devtoberfest after TechEd Virtual. And don't forget to enter into the contest. We have activities every day of the week, either by filling out validation tutorials for what you've just saw in your lecture sessions or completing tutorials that go along with them as well. So we've got lots of fun ways for you to earn points towards the big prize that you could win, win, which is, of course, a trip to one of the TechEd in-person events next year. And keep an eye out for TechEd on Tour, where from the ASUG TechConnect event, we're going to be bringing the winners from last year's Devtoberfest. We'll have some great pictures and videos from that event when it takes place in early November. So we look forward to seeing you in all the Devtoberfest and TechEd activities that are coming your way this month.

[Josh] Hey everybody. Are you as excited as I am that it is almost time for SAP TechEd? That's right. October 8th and 9th, SAP TechEd is happening this year. We're going to be coming to you live from Heidelberg, Germany. But if you can't be with us because it's virtual and you still want to talk with others and meet and watch the entire event, you can organize your own watch party. I know that several watch parties have already been organized for the SAP TechEd keynote, which is an hour and a half. It's going to be Michael Amling, Mohammed Alam, several others are going to be putting lots of great information out in this year's keynote. And right after that, for 30 minutes, me and Nora are going to get together and we're going to be hosting a community welcome.

So lots of watch parties are being organized around the world. The SAP community has several websites that I'll put links to in this video so you can see where others are getting together and maybe you can organize your own watch party as well. You can set it up, you can organize it on the SAP community website, and just let others know that you're going to be getting together to watch the Keynote and the Community Welcome and any other parts of SAP TechEd that you want to watch with others. I'll put a link to the one in Zurich, Hamburg, Sao Paulo, and then I think there's going to be one coming from Bengaluru, there's an SAP Inside Track, an SIT event that is happening on October 5th, and if you register for that, I believe you get an invite to the watch party, which is again happening on October 8th, when SAP's TechEd first day is going to go live at 9 a.m. Central European Time. I don't know where you are, so you have to figure out your own times on yourself. That's it, and I can't wait to see you from TechEd.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 27th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 30th.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be Techtoberfest - Use this opportunity to show off your coding setup. This could be your desk, office, or even your favorite development tools.
