entry.title: University of the People and SAP TechEd partner to empower the leaders of tomorrow
entry.content:
Brazilian educator and philosopher Paulo Freire once said, “Education does not change the world. Education changes people. People change the world.” However, with recent events like the COVID-19 pandemic, education access globally has been disrupted, with over 616 million students affected by school closures, risking an escalation in learning poverty where 50%, or approximately 825 million children worldwide, could lack basic literacy and numeracy skills by 2030. This crisis extends beyond immediate learning loss, impacting the future employability and innovative capabilities of today's youth.

At SAP, we know that empowering global learners to reach their highest potential by providing access to equitable education is the key to innovation and opportunity. We are proud to be part of the solution of the education and employment crisis, joining in the journey of so many learners as they look to use their education to make sustainable, positive impacts to change the world.

Since 2022, SAP has been a proud partner of  University of the People, the first nonprofit, tuition-free, American accredited, online university dedicated to global access to higher education. Established in 2009, the university provides virtual learning for more than 152,000 students worldwide, including over 18,000 refugees within its student population. University of the People serves students through the belief that higher education is a fundamental right for all. The virtual learning format of the university tackles the education crisis head on, providing education access for many students who don’t have a traditional brick-and-mortar University experience available due to various circumstances. Regardless of background, students are still able to complete their degrees, recognized by American accreditation standards. This sets up graduates for employment success and closes the education and employment gap for generations to come.

In celebration of SAP TechEd, we are excited to announce that SAP will be supporting 10 new scholarships for refugee women from around the world who are pursuing associate degrees in computer science at University of the People. These scholarships reinforce SAP’s commitment to University of the People and are a continuation of 10 scholarships awarded a year ago at SAP TechEd 2023. In addition to SAP TechEd, the company also broadly supports a full portfolio of scholarships in partnership with University of the People.

While these scholarships continue to build on our previous efforts, it's the personal stories of the students who benefit from this support that highlight the power of equitable education in action. One University of the People student, Kesanet Kidane from Eritrea, shares that she was inspired to think and dream big after seeing others around her unable to receive higher education. “I am a refugee in the US who was born and grew up in Eritrea. I wanted to see a world beyond the Eritrean border. I crossed the border at night hoping to see the other side of the world; the opportunist one. After waiting two years for my process in Ethiopia, I came to America four years ago for better opportunities in life and education.” After finishing high school, learning English, and living with her single mother and younger brother, Kesanet is eager to earn her degree in computer science while simultaneously working part-time. “After finishing at University of the People, I have a plan to get my master’s degree and get a job. Getting this scholarship is a path to fulfill my better education dream and a way to my better future.”

Another student, Maliha, was studying civil engineering at a university in Kabul, Afghanistan, when the Taliban took over the country. She shares her experience choosing University of the People to complete her education despite the challenges she faced during this difficult time. “I feared my dreams of earning a college degree had vanished forever. For the next six months, I hid in my home from the Taliban, not knowing what the future would bring.” After receiving a call from a former classmate informing her of the University of the People scholarships offered to Afghan women, Maliha immediately applied to finish her studies with a bachelor’s degree in computer science. “I was overjoyed that I could continue my education safely online from home. University of the People helped me to dream again about a better future and gave me the confidence to chase my dreams.” Recently, Maliha was accepted to a graduate program at Carnegie Mellon University and awarded a full scholarship. After traveling to Pakistan and receiving a visa to come to the United States, she was able to arrive safely and is excited to continue her studies at Carnegie Mellon.

Students like Kesanet and Maliha showcase how creating access to education for willing learners worldwide can be the first steppingstone to opportunity. Equipping students with the skills for tomorrow, especially while they are facing their own personal journeys of uprooting their lives for a better future, empowers confidence, inspires hope, and fuels innovation.

SAP is proud to support University of the People in celebration of SAP TechEd 2024, and to remain ongoing partners in the educational journeys for the changemakers of tomorrow. Learn more about SAP and University of the People’s partnership, along with our other education focused initiatives at SAP.

entry.title: Don’t Miss Database Sessions at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right around the corner and includes a dynamic lineup of keynote presentations and interactive jumpstart sessions across SAP’s Data and Analytics portfolio, including SAP HANA Cloud. For database enthusiasts who enjoy solving business challenges and empowering users with top-tier tools, be sure to register for the sessions highlighted later in this blog.


A Short Note About the Next Generation of Applications

Chances are, anyone reading this article is likely familiar with using, maintaining, or dealing with legacy applications. As these systems age, their support costs grow while business needs evolve beyond the application’s original scope. These issues often leave IT decision-makers in a difficult position, constantly weighing the benefits of maintaining the status quo against the need for innovation. The unfortunate and simple truth regarding legacy applications is that they are no longer adequate for today’s business needs. To bridge this gap, IT teams must transition to intelligent data applications and utilize the full power of SAP's in-memory database. Features such as data-tiering, multi-model engines, and AI libraries enable today's custom solutions to handle a new generation of business challenges.

At SAP TechEd 2024, four feature presentations showcase intelligent data applications using SAP HANA Cloud as the database. Learn more about these virtual sessions below and make sure to register today!


DA101 | SAP HANA Cloud: Recent innovations, road map, and strategy 
Tuesday, Oct 8, 11:30 AM - 11:55 AM CET / 5:30 AM - 5:55 AM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

As businesses innovate to meet evolving demands and drive efficiency, staying ahead of data infrastructure advancements is crucial. Join Thomas Hammer for an in-depth look at the latest innovations in SAP HANA Cloud, including new vector capabilities that integrate generative AI into enterprise solutions. This session also covers the latest road map and strategy, offering insights into creating a scalable, future-proof database platform with SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.


DA102 | AI-enabled clean core with SAP HANA Cloud 
Tuesday, Oct 8, 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CET / 9:30 AM - 9:55 AM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

Maintaining stable and consistent core ERP applications while driving innovation is a key challenge for many businesses today. In this session, Volker Saggau explains SAP’s clean core strategy and demonstrates how it helps global companies keep their ERP systems up-to-date and unmodified without comprising system integrity. With the support of SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model and multi-tenancy capabilities, SAP customer Henkel shares how they balance system hygiene with innovation by leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform to power their $12 billion revenue environment.


DA103 | Generative AI-driven business transformation enabled by SAP HANA Cloud
Wednesday, Oct 9, 6:30 PM - 6:55 PM CET / 12:30 PM - 12:55 PM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

As AI evolves, organizations need robust solutions for business transformation. Join Christoph Morgen and Markus Fath for a session that explores how SAP HANA Cloud supports the development of generative AI applications on the SAP Business Technology Platform. Key topics include multi-model capabilities like the vector engine, insights into SAP HANA’s AI function libraries, and a practical use case involving SAP Joule.


DA181 | Build AI-enabled intelligent data applications to help keep the core clean 
Wednesday, Oct 9, 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CET / 1:30 PM - 1:55 PM EST
Register  |  Watch Session Preview

In the pursuit of innovation, organizations should ensure their core ERP systems remain unmodified and consistent. This session discusses the concept of clean core and its role in supporting custom applications alongside their ERP systems. Key topics include how SAP HANA Cloud powers the development of AI-enabled intelligent data applications using its vector engine as well as the upcoming knowledge graph engine. The session also highlights how code generation within SAP Build speeds up the development process.


Build and Deploy Intelligent Data Applications Today

Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud. Supplement these SAP TechEd sessions with first-hand experience using the SAP HANA Cloud free basic trial. Click here to get started.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 3rd, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/03/sap-developer-news-october-3rd-2024/

DESCRIPTION

UI5 linter 1.0 and SAPUI5 Roadmap

  • Release of UI5 linter 1.0 https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/release-of-ui5-linter-v1-0/ba-p/13880556
  • SAPUI5 Roadmap on SAP Roadmap Explorer https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800001361&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q4%202024

Devtoberfest Week 2 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-2-contest-activities/ba-p/13880856
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d
  • Devtoberfest will be on pause next week for SAP TechEd Virtual. It will resume on October 14th.

SAP TechEd Virtual

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG

What's New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q3

  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – September 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-september-2024/ba-p/13873862
  • New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q3: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-machine-learning-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2024-q3/ba-p/13874878
  • Calculation View Features of 2024 QRC3: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-2024-qrc3/ba-p/13866974
  • What's New in SAP HANA Cloud Central | SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2495b34492334456a49084831c2bea4e?locale=en-US&Valid_as_Of=2024-09-01:2024-09-30
  •  Where can I acquire the skills to use monitor and performance tools within SAP HANA Cockpit?: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-blog-posts/where-can-i-acquire-the-skills-to-use-monitor-and-performance-tools-within/ba-p/13874201
  • What's New with SAP Datasphere in Q3, 2024? https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-community/what-s-new-with-sap-datasphere-in-q3-2024/ec-p/13808661#M638

SAP Partner ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

  • Academy Details (Partner Portal Access Required):  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2024/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00003900_Webinar_English.html?#accordion-panel-section-libraryItem-descriptionSection-accordioniteml

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 UI5 linter 1.0
1:47 SAPUI5 Roadmap
2:22 Devtoberfest Week 2 Recap
4:31 SAP TechEd Virtual
5:20 What's New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q3
7:33 SAP Partner ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 3rd, 2024.

[DJ] In any software project, there are best practices to which, ideally, developers will adhere. I am talking about things like code readability and maintainability, code standards and style guidelines, quality and security. Now, a linter is the ideal tool for checking for and gently but only enforcing these best practices. Now, back in March of this year, the UI5 linter was announced in a blog post from Oliver. Now, the initial version still had lots of gaps, but it was warmly received by the developer community, who gave lots of feedback, which was in turn gratefully received and incorporated into the tool. Now, earlier this week saw the official 1.0 release of the UI5 linter. This 1.0 release adds more problem detections including use of deprecated framework components and APIs and also old style manifests and deprecated library usage. Now, of course, the linter even at 1.0 is still not quite complete, but the team are working hard on the items which they've put onto a roadmap. All it takes to get going with the UI5 linter is a quick npm install global of the UI5 linter package and you're off to the races.

By the way, talking of roadmaps, the latest SAPUI5 roadmap has been released. This roadmap covers the developments that are planned for the rest of this year, but also for the first two quarters of 2025. The roadmap covers items in the area of SAP Fiori development, UX consistency, UI controls and more. So head on over to the road map area to check it out.

[Tom] I can't believe we've already covered two full weeks of Devtoberfest. It's been great to see so many people joining the educational sessions, completing tutorials, tracking their progress on the game board, and overall interacting with each other on the community. Now, after just the first five days of Devtoberfest, we already had over 20,000 unique viewers on the content of Devtoberfest. That's a great chunk of the community coming out to enjoy all this Devtoberfest content. And over half of those, 20,000, had never watched any video on the SAP Developers YouTube channel. So maybe we have some new viewers here watching the developer news for the first time. If so, that's you, welcome and I hope you stick around and enjoy more news that we bring you every week on this channel.

Now Devtoberfest is taking a slight pause here. We had the first two weeks of Devtoberfest. Next week is SAP TechEd. We have the virtual TechEd taking place on Tuesday and Wednesday. The developer advocates will be live from Germany doing the developer keynote deconstructed as well as bringing you special episodes of the developer news on this channel at the end of each of those days. There we'll recap the big announcements and other highlights for the TechEd event. But don't worry, then the week after TechEd, we're going to return with more Devtoberfest. We have two full more weeks of Devtoberfest content coming your way, and in those two weeks we can particularly cover in detail some of the things that were just announced at TechEd, so you have plenty more time to continue on the Devtoberfest contest. Maybe next week gives you a little chance to catch up on some of the videos that you missed, in addition to tuning in to check out TechEd. So we look forward to seeing you for more weeks of Devtoberfest, as well as the TechEd virtual live online next week. And to tell you more about TechEd over to you, you, Josh.

[Josh] We are days away from coming to you live from the App House in Heidelberg. It's going to be unbelievable. 90 plus sessions, all the developer advocates from around the world, tons of customer and expert speakers all under one roof. But you've got to sign up now. We'll have live keynote analysis plus live Q&A where you can get answers to your questions in real time or interact with a specific session speaker. You'll miss this chance to take in code demos, live lectures, and virtual workshops all designed to help you deepen your technical expertise and, most importantly, interact and have fun with our incredible community of thinkers and creators. So what are you waiting for? Sign up now and I'll see you online next week.

[Witalij] I'd like to share with you my summary of the top 5 features introduced in the Q3 2024 release of SAP HANA Cloud. First, SAP HANA Cloud now supports vector indexing, which is crucial for efficiently managing and querying large vector datasets. This feature enables faster and more efficient searches, particularly useful for machine learning and AI workloads. The release includes the addition of the HDB scan clustering algorithm enhancing the capabilities of unsupervised machine learning. This enhancement enables data scientists and application developers using SAP HANA Cloud among you to capture clusters in heterogeneous data and identify groups of similar objects or text documents based on embeddings in vector data. Third, in the SAP HANA native application web space for SAP Business Application Studio, You can now use the generative AI tools included in SAP Build Code to help you create SQL code based on the prompt you type into the console using natural language. It simplifies query construction and reduces time required for developing complex data analysis queries. Fourth, in the SAP HANA native application dev space for SAP Business Application Studio, it is now possible to use the SQL Notebooks to test your SQL code on SAP HANA Cloud database before you run it productively. And last but not least, there are a lot of important new features in calculation views modeling. And I would recommend reviewing detailed description of all of them in a separate blog post written by product managers. All these features focus on enhancing the efficiency, flexibility, and productivity of data management and analysis within SAP HANA Cloud, offering more advanced tools for developers and data scientists. And if you are looking for what's new in SAP DataSphere in Q3, then product management prepared a separate community call for you to be streamed on October 16th.

[Tom] This specific new segment is targeted right at our SAP partners. We have a webinar series coming up to help partners who are looking to do their ABAP Cloud certification. This is the certification back end  developer ABAP Cloud. And in particular, this webinar series is going to help you with content around core ABAP skills, as well as practicing clean core extensibility for SAP S4HANA. Now, this webinar series is coming up to cover all the materials that I mentioned. It is a multi-day, in fact, four-day webinar series with three to three and a half hours of content each day. This is coming up at the end of October, October 22nd, 23rd, 24th, and October 29th. You're interested in this kind of content to help you as a partner developer who's looking to update their skills for about Cloud, then check out the link in the description to the Parker Edge portal page with more information about this webinar series.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Você já conhece as trilhas de conteúdos ?
entry.content:
O SAP TechEd 2024 ocorrerá nos dias 08 e 09 de Outubro de 2024. 

E para orientar os usuários a ter um melhor aproveitamento dos conteúdos, a SAP disponibilizou trilhas de conteúdo. E cada trilha de conteúdo abrange um amplo espectro de produtos e soluções SAP, em formatos de sessão adaptados às suas necessidades.

Elas são:

🔵(DT) Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP

O SAP S/4HANA Cloud oferece suporte à transformação digital com soluções para todas as necessidades. Não importa se você está adotando seu primeiro ERP ou migrando de um existente, saiba por que o SAP S/4HANA Cloud oferece uma solução completa e modular com a IA e a análise necessárias para levar seu negócio adiante.

🔴(XP) SAP Business Technology Platform – General
Obtenha uma visão geral das principais tecnologias que compõem a plataforma, saiba como começar, ouça sobre orientações e práticas recomendadas e aprenda a operar e integrar seus aplicativos com segurança.

⚫(AD) Application Development and Automation

Aprenda a usar soluções SAP Build, infundidas com IA, para desenvolver aplicativos full-stack, automações e espaços de trabalho digitais. Acelere a modernização do ERP, promova a inovação e simplifique a automação, mantendo o núcleo limpo.

🟠 (DA) Data and Analytics

Obtenha acesso contínuo a dados empresariais de missão crítica, não importa onde eles estejam. Crie soluções que dão propósito aos dados, ao mesmo tempo em que simplificam o cenário de dados com tecido de dados empresariais, planejamento e análise estendidos e aplicativos de dados inteligentes.

🟢 (IN) Integration

Integre e automatize processos de negócios, APIs e eventos em aplicativos SAP e não SAP usando o SAP Integration Suite. Acelere a inovação com recursos assistidos por IA e harmonize experiências em diversos cenários.

⚪(AI) Artificial Intelligence
Descubra como inovações como Joule, nosso copiloto de IA generativa e modelos de linguagem grande estão transformando todas as áreas de negócios. Ouça como a IA pode aprimorar a tomada de decisões e a agilidade, ao mesmo tempo em que entrega resultados tangíveis.

Qual será seu caminho ? DT, XP, AD ... ?

Abraço,

Luiz Carlos de Souza

entry.title: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!
entry.content:
SAP Build was launched at SAP TechEd 2022 as a low-code suite on SAP Business Technology Platform, that allows developers and also those business users with less technical expertise to create enterprise applications, automate business processes and design business sites with drag-and-drop simplicity.

During the last two years, SAP Build has continuously evolved with a major milestone last year: SAP Build Code, announced at SAP TechEd 2023 and generally available since March this year, added pro-code capabilities to SAP Build. SAP Build Code is our turn-key development environment, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development and based on CAP development framework, that combines runtime and design time capabilities with built-in solutions for DevOps and Application Lifecycle Management.

Today marks another major milestone as we add ABAP environments to the SAP Build suite, allowing developers to work across their ABAP, Java/JavaScript and low-code projects from the same starting point, the SAP Build lobby, and delivering interoperability capabilities.

With SAP Build, customers can accelerate their ERP modernization, foster innovation and automate processes, all within a single comprehensive suite of tools for extension development which includes low-code- pro-code and generative AI capabilities. Read more with use cases by LoB in our e-book "Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build". Deep dive into the clean core topic with our whitepaper: "Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud".

 

ABAP Environments now part of SAP Build – What does it mean?

With the inclusion of ABAP environments in SAP Build, developers can now create new ABAP Cloud projects, or work on existing ones, from the SAP Build lobby and then navigate to their ABAP development environment – the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT). Developers will also be able to monitor their ABAP projects from the lobby, right next to their other SAP Build projects. This includes ABAP projects developed on both the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP environment – also known as ‘on stack extensibility’ – as well as the SAP BTP ABAP environment – also known as ‘side-by-side extensibility’.

 And there’s more: by including ABAP environments into SAP Build, we’ve made it easier for developers to seamlessly combine ABAP development artifacts with frontend artifacts for SAP Fiori and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). For instance, you can now use a BAPI that is available in your on-premise ERP or S/4HANA private cloud, making developing CAP applications easier to develop with this new capability.

 

Let me walk you through a few concrete examples of fusion development projects showing different types of extensions: use cases 1 and 2 are side-by-side extensions created fully on SAP BTP, and use cases 3 and 4 and on-stack extensions relying partially or totally on S/4HANA:

 

In use case 1, the back end of the extension is created as a CAP service by a pro-developer using SAP Build Code, while the front end with the UI and some business logic is created by a key user with SAP Build Apps.

In use case 2, the extension is a custom process, created by a business user with SAP Build Process Automation. At some point, the workflow of this process is calling a specific ABAP Cloud service on SAP BTP, created by an ABAP Developer using ABAP ADT for Eclipse.

In use case 3, a specific ABAP event is created by an ABAP developer with ABAP ADT for Eclipse. When it occurs, this event triggers a process including automations, created by a business user with SAP Build Process Automation.

In use case 4, the back end of the extension is made by an ABAP Developer using ABAP ADT for Eclipse. As this tool does not handle UI technologies, the custom UI for the extension is created by a UI developer using SAP Build Code.

These examples are demonstrating that we are at the beginning of a journey to bring the ABAP- and non-ABAP worlds together, making SAP Build the go-to offering for ERP extensibility in the market. The next quarters, we’ll be releasing more functionalities to make the tools more and more interoperable.

By providing ABAP development capabilities in SAP Build, SAP allows customers and partners to accelerate ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate, all within a single suite of AI driven tools for ABAP, CAP and low-code based development.

Blurring the lines between S/4HANA and SAP Build: the Extensibility Wizard

The ABAP- and non-ABAP world also converge directly within the ERP: The Extensibility Wizard is an embedded experience in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud allowing key users to extend business applications – both on stack and side by side on SAP BTP – directly out of the ERP without losing context.

Today, customers can extend custom fields and custom logic on S/4 HANA, and processes in SAP Build. Soon, the option of creating Custom Fiori Apps and UI5 Adaptation from SAP Build Code will also be available.

This wizard can be accessed from any extensible SAP S/4HANA app by clicking the "Create Extension" option available in the User menu, on top right of the screen. Clicking this “Create Extension” option, the Extensibility Wizard opens up, displaying the various extension options:

For more details on how to set up the Extensibility Wizard and what extensions options are available, please read this blog post: “SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition”.

SAP Build powered by Joule’s generative AI capabilities

SAP Joule is available at hand to take action and optimize your ABAP and Build Code and Build low-code development, leveraging Generative AI capabilities across the SAP Build suite. We’re excited to announce lots of new Joule capabilities across SAP Build.

To discover the power of Joule in SAP Build to accelerate your development process and the AI features in SAP Build that improve user productivity, watch SAP TechEd session AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build with Marc Huber and Rupam Bhattacharya.

We have introduced many new generative AI powered capabilities to support low-code and pro-code application development and automations:

SAP Build and AI

  • In SAP Build Apps, Joule ​allows to auto-generate pages to handle data entities, and also to generate formulas and translations. SAP Build Apps also brings now a feature for automatic sample data generation.
  • Process Automation is also greatly improved by GenAI-based generation and editing of process automations. You are able to describe your process in natural language, to interact and iterate to finalize desired process flow and finally generate the process with automations, forms, and decisions (business policies) that fits your requirement. Joule can also create a short comprehensive description of complex historical or newly generated complex artifacts, for instance the summary of a complex decision table. And gen-AI powered Intelligent Document Processing​ allows to simplify the creation of document-based business processes.
  • Intelligent assistance is empowering business sites, with seamless Joule access within SAP Build Work Zone​. AI responses grounded in Work Zone content​ allow AI-driven content creation and summarization​, and intelligent decision recommendation in Task Center.​

SAP Build Code and AI

Writing code is hard and requires upfront learning. Even experienced developers regularly need to consult the documentation which slows down the development process.​ And test data creation is tedious: one must always ensure that data fits the model and is up to date​.

New capabilities include code explanation, search results from SAP documentation, and enhancements to develop SAP Fiori and mobile applications. To cater to developers who prefer to use Visual Studio Code, SAP has released a Visual Studio Code extension for Joule, giving them access to SAP Joule directly from their preferred development environment.

Joule-powered Generative AI capabilities in SAP Build Code allow to

  • Create all necessary CDS files which define the entities, relations and service projections according to a prompt
  • Generate business logic and SAP Fiori UIs, with also sample data
  • Generate unit tests for business in services, with automated unit test coverage ​

Even existing models can be changed, just by providing descriptions​.

Generative AI for ABAP development

With new ABAP capabilities, Joule can now help ABAP developers be more efficient with their development tasks. Joule leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for ABAP code generation that SAP has trained with more than 150 million lines of code. These LLMs generate high-quality ABAP code and code explanations. With this model, Joule reduces development time for both new and experienced ABAP developers and helps customers better understand their old ABAP extensions, accelerating their migration to a clean core.

Furthermore, ABAP developers can now access the Generative AI Hub, utilizing leading open-source and proprietary AI models, including LLMs, to infuse custom AI capabilities into their ABAP-based extensions.

In a few words, with Joule in ABAP development tools, developers can

  • streamline the development process of creating Business objects with Joule copilot
  • Increase efficiency by decoding legacy ABAP code and accelerating migration to a cloud compliant code.
  • use Joule copilot for ABAP to generate application logic, create data models, sample data and unit test cases from natural language descriptions.
  • improve understanding and maintainability by using Joule copilot to develop ABAP documentation.
  • maintain quality and minimize risk using Joule copilot to do code review for transports.

 

Joule Studio in SAP Build

Finally, I’m also very excited to announce Joule studio, that allows developers to enhance Joule’s out-of-the-box capabilities to support organization-specific workflows. Developers will be able to navigate to Joule studio directly from the SAP Build lobby and create, deploy, monitor and manage their own Joule skills with drag-and-drop simplicity, and integrate with all SAP and any third-party system for an entirely integrated conversational user experience. To learn more about Joule studio in SAP Build, watch SAP TechEd session AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills with Maria Kondratyeva and Miguel Angel Meza Martinez.

With these new capabilities we just announced, developers can now unlock new levels of efficiency with the latest Joule-powered AI capabilities for SAP Build. Uniquely trained on SAP data and processes, Joule helps developers write code and design workflows for SAP-based development across ABAP, CAP, and low-code tools.

And SAP Build is also the solution to extend Joule with custom skills, using the Joule studio in SAP Build.

Key Takeaways

To summarize what we announced at SAP TechEd 2024:

  1. Increase the pace of creating applications with Joule copilot capabilities: You will generate high quality ABAP code and help developers understand and analyze ABAP extensions using Large Language Model trained by SAP.
  2. Streamline cloud migration with the ABAP Cloud Development Model: You will seamlessly extend SAP environments to create scalable, secure, and stable extensions which are cloud ready.
  3. Create future-proof, clean core extensions with smooth interoperability between on stack and side by side extensions: you will foster collaboration between developers to quickly create critical extensions, easily navigating between the ABAP Environments and other SAP Build solutions with complete context.

More than ever, SAP Build is THE extensibility solution in an SAP context: with SAP Build including ABAP Cloud, you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development.

Become part of our SAP Builders community and try out SAP Build. Take our SAP Build Learning Journeys.

The future is yours!

entry.title: SAP TechEd Keynote 2024 – Technology Highlights
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 kicked off virtually, bringing you a lot of new exciting technology updates. In this highlights blog post, I have compiled a quick summary of the announcements and updates shared by Michael Ameling, Chief Product Officer, SAP Business Technology Platform, to help you navigate everything that is new around SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

BTP General:

Let’s start with some of the general updates on SAP BTP.

First and foremost, SAP is expanding BTP Coverage in the next years via eight SAP-managed data centers.

I am sure, you are all aware of SAP Discovery Center is the central place to go for businesses looking to adopt SAP’s cloud solutions efficiently with the help of multiple mission-based learnings. These missions are structured guides that walk users through specific use cases and help users achieve business goals using BTP services.

SAP now announced for the SAP Discovery Center the option to deploy some of the missions with a Quick-Account-Setup, helping users to get the exact technical BTP set-up as per the mission at the click of a button. Check out the quick demo below for more insights.

Quick Account Set-up 

For expert technical users, who would like to customize these missions, you will get the ready Terraform based automation scripts, which are behind QAS, publicly available here - https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-terraform-samples/tree/main/released/discovery_center 

Application Development and Automation:

As an SAP Cloud ERP customer, it’s paramount to follow a “Clean Core” strategy to be able to receive new innovations from SAP quickly. SAP is strengthening its SAP Build portfolio to support you when building extensions, ensuring your system stays consistent, maintainable, and upgradeable.

SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud. With that, SAP Build becomes THE extension solution for all your needs.

By bringing ABAP cloud capabilities into SAP Build, you will also be able to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby. Watch this short demo to see SAP Build lobby in action.

SAP Build in Action 

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, SAP announced the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard which helps both, key users and developers create both on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

Watch this short demo to get some insights.

Custom Field creation via Joule in SAP S/4HANA 

Data and Analytics:

I am sure you remember one of SAP’s biggest announcements from SAP TechEd 2023, which was the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine.

Since last year, SAP has made HANA Cloud Vector Engine a thousand times faster. Now SAP is adding a native in-database vectorization with its own SAP-specific text embedding model, which simplifies the creation of vector embeddings significantly.

Now SAP announced SAP Knowledge graph and SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge graph engine, which allows customers to build business and intelligent data applications like never before.

By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

For example, in areas like Supply Chain Management, the Knowledge Graph Engine can help you identify suppliers based on their carbon footprint. But the real value is: 

You can now factor-in any criteria that might impact your supplier selection. These could be geopolitical risks or weather conditions. The Knowledge Graph engine can give you a holistic view across existing data silos.

Both, the vector engine as well as the knowledge graph engine, are fully integrated with other existing HANA Cloud multi-model data processing capabilities.  

Stay tuned to learn more as both SAP Knowledge graph and SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge graph engine will be available in Q1 2025

SAP is making it easier for everyone to create intelligent data applications by introducing Generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud which is available via SAP Build. It allows anyone to perform common data analysis steps and handle complex machine learning operations within SAP Build code also with the help of our AI co-pilot Joule. GenAI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud will be available from Q1 2025.

Check out this demo to see it in action

SAP HANA Cloud Generative AI Toolkit in Action 

SAP also announced new data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere, which allows any organization to implement business data fabric in a simple, cost-efficient and scalable manner. The new data lake capabilities are available from Q4 2024.

And: SAP is making the integration of analytics easier for you.

With the introduction of new CDS annotations, you can now use the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model to directly embed advanced analytics into your transactional applications on BTP.

SAP also introduced SAP Analytics Cloud compass. With that, you can model complex risk scenarios through the Monte Carlo simulation method. The business user friendly interface allows you to perform real-time analysis without prior setup or knowledge.

With technology continuously evolving, SAP has also brought SAP Analytics Cloud to Apple Vision Pro. See some impressions here:

SAP Analytics Cloud in Apple Vision Pro 

Integration:

SAP Integration Suite offers hundreds of connectors, thousands of pre-built integrations and embedded AI to support ALL your integration scenarios.

Early this year, SAP announced the Generative AI-based Integration Flow generation. Now, to complement that, SAP previewed the new AI capability which allows customers to optimize their running Integration Suite custom scripts in areas like memory.

Using the “Inspect” feature of SAP Integration Suite, administrators can identify the need for optimization and generate proposals including entire groovy script without writing a single line of code. See how it works in this short demo:

SAP Integration Suite AI-Script Optimization 

This feature will be available as part of the SAP Integration Suite, premium edition, in H1 2025.

SAP also announced the introduction of new Integration adapters for Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake and many more already available from Q4 2024.

And to support more mid-sized customers, SAP announced the introduction of a new license for SAP SuccessFactors and SAP GROW customers: The SAP integration Suite, starter edition, available from Q4 2024.

Artificial Intelligence:

No technology keynote is complete today without talking about AI. So, here we go.

SAP is integrating AI into your everyday work through the AI Foundation on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Customers can leverage the broadest selection of foundational models from top providers all in one place through the Generative AI Hub with the benefit of one unified legal and commercial framework.

While Joule is getting integrated into multiple SAP solutions, we are making Joule even more flexible and customizable with the introduction of Joule studio in SAP Build.

Joule studio allows partners and customers to create and deploy custom Joule skills.

And that’s not all: Joule also goes agentic. With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration. Learn more about it here:

Joule goes Agentic 

Joule now brings ABAP developer capabilities inside of ABAP in Eclipse, based on SAP’s own ABAP Large Language Model (LLM). ABAP LLM has been trained on over 250 million lines of code from SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It’s available now for selected customers and partners in an early adopter version and will be generally available in Q1 2025.

While Joule is just only one year old, SAP will already have infused 80% of the most used workflows in Joule by the end of this year. SAP also announced Joule for mobile to support you while you are on the move, which will be available in SAP Start during Q4 2024 and in other applications early next year.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While this is only a summary of the key technology highlights, I would encourage you to watch the full keynote here: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual.html

And to learn more about all the new innovations and product updates, check out our TechEd News Guide:

https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html  

Have fun at SAP TechEd 2024!

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 1
entry.content:
Description

SAP TechEd General

  • News Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html
  • TechEd Watch Live: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Build Code Enhancements for Developers

  • Application Development and Automation News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/application-development-and-automation.html
  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • SAP Build Code Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241008&search.offeringsproducts=1698907352855001TA5c

AI Innovations – Part 1

  • Artificial Intelligence News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html
  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

AI Innovations – Part 2

  • Artificial Intelligence News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html
  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:09 Build Code Enhancements for Developers
3:30 AI Innovations – Part 1
4:33 AI Innovations – Part 2

Transcript

[Intro] Well, a developer in his houseboat, innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't.

[DJ] So while the crew were at lunch we've snuck into the studios to record this item on the couch. So we're at TechEd as we can see here I've got some a card here and one of the items announced at TechEd as you may know is that there are new capabilities that have been added and will be added to SAP Build the complete solution for building end-to-end extensions on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and on other line of business applications. There are three here written down that I want to highlight.

There's a new extension wizard and it allows you to jump directly from SAP S/4 HANA Cloud public edition straight to SAP Build. Why is that important? Because there's a lot of context within the context of the application and that context objects events and so on business data is super important not to lose that so this new extension wizard which is available for building side-by-side and on stack extensions allows that jump including the context there are new generative AI capabilities with dual coming in this quarter for example alongside the already existing code generation facilities, there are or there will be code explanation facilities and also document search. I think they're great complements to what already exists.

On the low code arena, there are also dual enhancements to help with the creation of UIs and workflow automation.

And last but not least, there's now direct access or there will be, by the end of this year, direct access from SAP Build to ABAP development environments in the cloud and on BTP. So basically the whole idea is that this is one step closer to a more unified development environment. You can jump from the lobby to SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps and now to specific ABAP development environments and this helps enormously with success for teams especially in fusion development contexts. Anyway check out the announcements from SAP TechEd for more details on these new SAP build capabilities.

[Nora] There's a lot happening with AI at TechEd at the moment and you've heard a lot of news and I'm just going to try to put as much as possible into the next couple of minutes. So there has been a lot of rumor on the SAP Foundation model for a while now and it's getting real and if you want to know more about the SAP Foundation model you should definitely check out the session AI 102 on Wednesday. SAP Foundation Model and Knowledge Graph, where you can learn about the SAP Foundation Model and how it can actually interpret structured data and fill in missing data points. Then we also have a lot of new features in the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. Mainly we have new models coming up, so we will have the IBM Granite model series. We will also have the Dali 3 model and the Amazon Titan image generator model. Those will all be available until the end of 2024. We also have other new features of Generative AI Hub, for example, the orchestration capability, including prompt templates and data masking. And then we also have some new features with Joule and Kevin is going to tell you more about that.

[Kevin] SAP is going to expand and elevate Joule's capabilities to drive business innovation by providing SAP Joule Studio to create custom skills for Joule, but it also will integrate Joule with SAP HANA Cloud to help you generate code for SAP HANA Cloud and help you work easier with SAP HANA Cloud as well. The SAP Cloud SDK for AI was available for Python already, but now we got that for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, helping you to integrate the capabilities of SAP Generative AI Hub of SAP AI Core directly into your business enterprise applications.
